name: DevFlow entry
purpose: Stable portable AI entrypoint. Use devflow query route/current/skills/rules first, then load source markdown/rules/skills only when selected by query results. Do not rely on home-level compatibility files.
workflowOrder: Use devflow query current for resume/current-task requests.
Use devflow-init after install to collect local profile, projects, scenes, skills, and rules.

For L3/L4 work or requests backed by Jira, Notion, Figma, PRD, or cross-project acceptance criteria, use OpenSpec as the optional spec-driven source of truth before implementation; do not load it for small L1/L2 edits unless already selected.

Carry forward each gate's concrete output as the input to the next gate: intake informs discovery, discovery informs plan/spec, plan/spec informs implementation, implementation evidence informs integration, integration evidence informs acceptance, and acceptance informs run/archive.
Use devflow query route to select only the context needed for the current task.
When work should survive the chat, create or update task state with devflow task start/update/finish before implementation.
defaultReadOrder: devflow query current for resume/current-task requests
devflow query route "<user request>" for project, task, scene, Workset, or panel routing
devflow query skills only when skill selection is needed
devflow query rules only when rule lookup is needed
readPaths, skills.sourcePath, and rules.sourcePath returned by query results only when selected
bundles/skills/devflow-init/SKILL.md when the user is onboarding or initializing DevFlow

routingDecision.projectsIndex: Run devflow query route "<user request>" to get candidate projects, scores, Workset, and selected readPaths.
routingDecision.projectDetail: After query selects a project, load only returned readPaths and any selected skills.sourcePath or rules.sourcePath.
routingDecision.scenesIndex: Use devflow query route when the task may involve a workflow scene or multiple projects.
routingDecision.sceneDetail: After query selects a scene, use the returned scene template, Workset, readPaths, and rule source paths.
routingDecision.fallback: If no project or scene is clear, ask one concise clarification instead of reading all source documents.

sourceReadPolicy.homeCompatibility: Do not read or require home-level compatibility files by default. Treat this repository entry and DevFlow query results as the portable source of truth.
sourceReadPolicy.projectDocs: Read the selected project's distributed entry file, usually <project>/.ai-configs/project.md, only when query results return it or the project summary is insufficient.
sourceReadPolicy.scenes: Read docs/scenes/*.md only when query results select a scene and detailed workflow text is needed.
sourceReadPolicy.rules: Run devflow query rules for rule inventory. Active rule sources under bundles/rules/** are loaded only when selected by query results, project, scene, or task gate.
sourceReadPolicy.skills: Run devflow query skills for skill inventory. Active bundles/skills/** are loaded only when query results, task state, or an explicit user request triggers the skill.
sourceReadPolicy.openspec: Read or create OpenSpec only when the task is L3/L4, has explicit Jira/Notion/Figma/PRD/spec input, crosses projects/devices, or needs durable acceptance criteria. Store only the OpenSpec change id, path, status, and handoff summary in task state.
sourceReadPolicy.profile: A fresh install has no long profile. Run devflow-init to create local profile details.

primaryInstallFlow: ask the AI tool to run devflow-init

workflowDependencies.purpose: Optional spec-driven source of truth for L3/L4, Jira/Notion/Figma/PRD-backed, cross-project, cross-device, or high-risk tasks.
Execution discipline layer for brainstorming, TDD, debugging, planning, verification, review, and branch finishing.

installation.globalEntrypointsStatus: deprecated; do not create home-level AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, or WORK_CONTEXT.md by default
installation.afterInstall: Ask the AI tool to run the devflow-init skill to initialize profile, projects, scenes, skills, and rules.

cli.internalScriptStatus: setup and doctor are retained for tests, CI, and low-level troubleshooting only; normal users should run devflow init.
cli.createsLocalCheckout: When run outside an existing devflow checkout, devflow init creates ./devflow from the npm package template. Use --dir <path> to choose another location.

taskFlow.defaultBehavior: Fresh install has no active task. Create task JSON only when the user starts work that should survive the chat.

specLayer.defaultUse: optional for L3/L4 and explicit spec-backed work; skipped for routine L1/L2 edits
specLayer.archivePolicy: At G7, completed OpenSpec changes should be synced or archived so delta specs merge into openspec/specs/ and the completed change moves to openspec/changes/archive/. Record lessons learned as a compact task note or follow-up artifact before archive when they should influence future work.

taskFlow.handoffPolicy: Every gate should leave a compact artifact or note that the next gate consumes. Do not add gstack or ce as default tools; only borrow the handoff discipline of feeding each stage's output into the next stage.
